Additional Considerations for Hinchliffe Stadium, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–291, div. B, title XXX, § 3037(b)128 Stat. 3783Dec. 19, 2014“(1).—In generalIn administering the approximately 6 acres of land containing Hinchliffe Stadium and generally depicted as the ‘Boundary Modification Area’ on the map entitled ‘Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park, Proposed Boundary Modification’, numbered T03/120,155, and dated April 2014, the Secretary of the Interior—“(A)may not include non-Federal property within the approximately 6 acres of land as part of Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park without the written consent of the owner;“(B)may not acquire by condemnation any land or interests in land within the approximately 6 acres of land; and“(C)shall not construe the inclusion of Hinchliffe Stadium made by this section to create buffer zones outside the boundaries of the Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park.“(2).—Outside activitiesThe fact that activities can be seen or head [sic, probably should be “heard”] from within the approximately 6 acres of land described in paragraph (1) shall not preclude such activities outside the boundary of the Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park.”

children.heading: City
children.label: 1 City
children.text: The term “City” means the City of Paterson, New Jersey.

children.heading: Commission
children.label: 2 Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park Advisory Commission established by subsection (e)(1).

children.heading: Historic District
children.label: 3 Historic District
children.text: The term “Historic District” means the Great Falls Historic District in the State.

children.heading: Management plan
children.label: 4 Management plan
children.text: The term “management plan” means the management plan for the Park developed under subsection (d).

children.heading: Map
children.label: 5 Map
children.text: The term “Map” means the map entitled “Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park–Proposed Boundary”, numbered T03/80,001, and dated May 2008.

children.heading: Park
children.label: 6 Park
children.text: The term “Park” means the Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park established by subsection (b)(1)(A).

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 7 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: State
children.label: 8 State
children.text: The term “State” means the State of New Jersey.

children.heading: Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park
children.label: b Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), there is established in the State a unit of the National Park System to be known as the “Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park”.

children.label: I
children.text: the Secretary has acquired sufficient land or an interest in land within the boundary of the Park to constitute a manageable unit; or

children.label: aa
children.text: the Great Falls State Park, including facilities for Park administration and visitor services; or

children.label: bb
children.text: any portion of the Great Falls State Park agreed to between the Secretary and the State or City; and

children.label: I
children.text: land owned by the State, City, or other public entity within the Historic District will be managed consistent with this section; and

children.label: II
children.text: future uses of land within the Historic District will be compatible with the designation of the Park.

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 2 Purpose
children.text: The purpose of the Park is to preserve and interpret for the benefit of present and future generations certain historical, cultural, and natural resources associated with the Historic District.

children.label: i
children.text: The upper, middle, and lower raceways.

children.label: ii
children.text: Mary Ellen Kramer (Great Falls) Park and adjacent land owned by the City.

children.label: iii
children.text: A portion of Upper Raceway Park, including the Ivanhoe Wheelhouse and the Society for Establishing Useful Manufactures Gatehouse.

children.label: iv
children.text: Overlook Park and adjacent land, including the Society for Establishing Useful Manufactures Hydroelectric Plant and Administration Building.

children.label: v
children.text: The Allied Textile Printing site, including the Colt Gun Mill ruins, Mallory Mill ruins, Waverly Mill ruins, and Todd Mill ruins.

children.label: vi
children.text: The Rogers Locomotive Company Erecting Shop, including the Paterson Museum.

children.label: vii
children.text: The Great Falls Visitor Center.

children.label: B
children.text: In addition to the lands described in subparagraph (A), the Park shall include the approximately 6 acres of land containing Hinchliffe Stadium and generally depicted as the “Boundary Modification Area” on the map entitled “Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park, Proposed Boundary Modification”, numbered T03/120,155A, and dated August 2015, which shall be administered as part of the Park in accordance with subsection (c)(1) and section 3 of the Hinchliffe Stadium Heritage Act.

children.heading: Availability of map
children.label: 4 Availability of map
children.text: The Map and the map referred to in paragraph (3)(B) shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the National Park Service.

children.heading: Publication of notice
children.label: 5 Publication of notice
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date on which the conditions in clauses (i) and (ii) of paragraph (1)(B) are satisfied, the Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register notice of the establishment of the Park, including an official boundary map for the Park.

children.label: A
children.text: this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: the National Park Service Organic Act ( et seq.); and16 U.S.C. 11See References in Text note below.1

children.label: ii
children.text: the Act of ( et seq.).August 21, 193516 U.S.C. 4611

children.label: A
children.text: to exercise civil and criminal jurisdiction; or

children.label: B
children.text: to carry out State laws (including regulations) and rules on non-Federal land located within the boundary of the Park.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: As the Secretary determines to be appropriate to carry out this section, the Secretary may enter into cooperative agreements with the owner of the Great Falls Visitor Center or any nationally significant properties within the boundary of the Park under which the Secretary may identify, interpret, restore, and provide technical assistance for the preservation of the properties.

children.label: i
children.text: conducting visitors through the properties; and

children.label: ii
children.text: interpreting the properties for the public.

children.heading: Changes or alterations
children.label: C Changes or alterations
children.text: No changes or alterations shall be made to any properties covered by a cooperative agreement entered into under subparagraph (A) unless the Secretary and the other party to the agreement agree to the changes or alterations.

children.label: i
children.text: the amounts made available to the project by the United States; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the portion of the increased value of the project attributable to the amounts made available under this paragraph, as determined at the time of the conversion, use, or, disposal.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: As a condition of the receipt of funds under this paragraph, the Secretary shall require that any Federal funds made available under a cooperative agreement shall be matched on a 1-to-1 basis by non-Federal funds.

children.heading: Form
children.label: ii Form
children.text: With the approval of the Secretary, the non-Federal share required under clause (i) may be in the form of donated property, goods, or services from a non-Federal source.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraphs (B) and (C), the Secretary may acquire land or interests in land within the boundary of the Park by donation, purchase from a willing seller with donated or appropriated funds, or exchange.

children.heading: Donation of State owned land
children.label: B Donation of State owned land
children.text: Land or interests in land owned by the State or any political subdivision of the State may only be acquired by donation.

children.heading: Hinchliffe Stadium
children.label: C Hinchliffe Stadium
children.text: The Secretary may not acquire fee title to Hinchliffe Stadium, but may acquire a preservation easement in Hinchliffe Stadium if the Secretary determines that doing so will facilitate resource protection of the stadium.

children.heading: Technical assistance and public interpretation
children.label: 5 Technical assistance and public interpretation
children.text: The Secretary may provide technical assistance and public interpretation of related historic and cultural resources within the boundary of the Historic District.

children.label: A
children.text: ; andsection 100502 of title 54

children.label: B
children.text: other applicable laws.

children.heading: Cost share
children.label: 2 Cost share
children.text: The management plan shall include provisions that identify costs to be shared by the Federal Government, the State, and the City, and other public or private entities or individuals for necessary capital improvements to, and maintenance and operations of, the Park.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park Advisory Commission
children.label: e Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park Advisory Commission

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established a commission to be known as the “Paterson Great Falls National Historical Park Advisory Commission”.

children.heading: Duties
children.label: 2 Duties
children.text: The duties of the Commission shall be to advise the Secretary in the development and implementation of the management plan.

children.label: i
children.text: 4 members shall be appointed after consideration of recommendations submitted by the Governor of the State;

children.label: ii
children.text: 2 members shall be appointed after consideration of recommendations submitted by the City Council of Paterson, New Jersey;

children.label: iii
children.text: 1 member shall be appointed after consideration of recommendations submitted by the Board of Chosen Freeholders of Passaic County, New Jersey; and

children.label: iv
children.text: 2 members shall have experience with national parks and historic preservation.

children.label: i
children.text: the date that is 30 days after the date on which the Secretary has received all of the recommendations for appointments under subparagraph (A); or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date that is 30 days after the Park is established in accordance with subsection (b).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A member shall be appointed for a term of 3 years.

children.heading: Reappointment
children.label: ii Reappointment
children.text: A member may be reappointed for not more than 1 additional term.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: B Vacancies
children.text: A vacancy on the Commission shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointment was made.

children.label: A
children.text: the Chairperson; or

children.label: B
children.text: a majority of the members of the Commission.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: 6 Quorum
children.text: A majority of the Commission shall constitute a quorum.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Commission shall select a Chairperson and Vice Chairperson from among the members of the Commission.

children.heading: Vice Chairperson
children.label: B Vice Chairperson
children.text: The Vice Chairperson shall serve as Chairperson in the absence of the Chairperson.

children.heading: Term
children.label: C Term
children.text: A member may serve as Chairperson or Vice Chairman for not more than 1 year in each office.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Members of the Commission shall serve without compensation.

children.heading: Travel expenses
children.label: ii Travel expenses
children.text: Members of the Commission shall be allowed travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, at rates authorized for an employee of an agency under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5, while away from the home or regular place of business of the member in the performance of the duties of the Commission.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide the Commission with any staff members and technical assistance that the Secretary, after consultation with the Commission, determines to be appropriate to enable the Commission to carry out the duties of the Commission.

children.label: I
children.text: the State;

children.label: II
children.text: any political subdivision of the State; or

children.label: III
children.text: any entity represented on the Commission.

children.heading: Nonapplicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: 9 Nonapplicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: shall not apply to the Commission.Section 1013(b) of title 5

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 10 Termination
children.text: The Commission shall terminate 10 years after .March 30, 2009

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 3 fiscal years after the date on which funds are made available to carry out this section, the Secretary shall complete a study regarding the preservation and interpretation of Hinchliffe Stadium, which is listed on the National Register of Historic Places.

children.label: A
children.text: the potential for listing the stadium as a National Historic Landmark; and

children.label: B
children.text: options for maintaining the historic integrity of Hinchliffe Stadium.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: g Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this section.
